cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer here. The question is asking what Lao Yang hoped regarding his article. Let me read through the story again to get the context right. So, Lao Yang was always interested in reading and writing. When he was younger, he wanted to be a writer because he thought writers were respected and made a lot of money. He submitted stories to editorial departments but never got any responses. Now he works in a factory and is busy, but he still reads when he can. He remembers his childhood dream of being a writer. When his daughter needs to write an article for school titled "My Father," he helps her by writing it himself. He finishes it quickly, confident that the teacher would like it. But then he finds out that the teacher said the article digressed from the subject. Lao Yang is confused and angry because he thought he was writing about his father. The question is about what Lao Yang hoped. The options are: A) his article could surprise the teacher B) his article could be chosen C) the children could like his article D) everyone could soon know him
